perturbation.original_text: In the event this Agreement is terminated, and the disclosing party so requests, the receiving party shall promptly return or destroy (and certify destruction of) all Confidential Information which it received from the disclosing party along with all copies which it made.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event this Agreement is terminated, the receiving party should consider returning or destroying Confidential Information at its discretion.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall promptly return or destroy' to 'should consider returning or destroying at its discretion,' the mandatory obligation to return or destroy confidential information upon termination is removed. This weakens the protection of confidential information and introduces ambiguity. The word 'shall' is a critical component of obligation in any agreement. By replacing that with the word 'should' introduces an in-text contradiction, because in the beginning of the agreement there is an expectation that both parties will honor the confidentality agreement. By making the destruction/returning of the copies discretionary, the receiver could be at risk of breaking the confidentality terms if that is not done. Which creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The receiving party shall not disclose Confidential Information to any third party without the prior written approval of the disclosing party. The receiving party shall maintain the Confidential Information with at least the same degree of care that the receiving party uses to protect its own similar categories of confidential and proprietary information, but no less than a reasonable degree of care under the circumstances. The receiving party may make copies of Confidential Information as deemed necessary. The receiving party is encouraged to label the copies accordingly.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces a mandatory requirement ('shall promptly return or destroy') with a discretionary suggestion ('should consider returning or destroying at its discretion'). This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states 'The receiving party shall not disclose Confidential Information to any third party...' and 'The receiving party shall maintain the Confidential Information...'. The original text ensured confidential information was protected after termination by requiring its return or destruction. The changed text allows the receiving party to choose whether or not to do so, potentially leading to a breach of confidentiality as described in the contradicted text. Thus, the change introduces a strong in-text contradiction because the contradicted text assumes the return or destruction of confidential information to prevent disclosure, an assumption invalidated by the changed text, which makes this action optional. Therefore, this is classified as an Omissions - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement, any accompanying CITR and CITRs executed from time to time hereafter which incorporate the terms of this Agreement constitute the entire agreement, written or verbal, between the parties with respect to the disclosure(s) of Confidential Information described in each CITR.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement, written or verbal, between the parties with respect to the disclosure(s) of Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the reference to the CITR, it removes the clarity of what constitutes the 'entire agreement.' This is an omission that causes an in-text contradiction. Without the CITR clarification, it creates uncertainty as to which specific disclosures are covered under the original agreement. If the definition of confidential information is removed as it pertains to the CITR, then other sections referencing the CITR are going to be contradicted and are no longer valid.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The confidential, proprietary and trade secret information of the disclosing party (hereinafter "Confidential Information") provided hereunder, is that information described in the Confidential Information Transmittal Record (CITR) form executed from time to time hereafter. CITR's are subject to the terms of this Agreement and shall be executed by the parties prior to the disclosure of Confidential Information. All information described in a CITR and marked with a "confidential," "proprietary," or similar legend shall be deemed Confidential Information. All Confidential Information received from the disclosing party shall be in tangible form. The CITR shall set forth the disclosing party, a description of the Confidential Information disclosed, the names of the representatives of the parties and the date when the disclosure covered by the CITR commenced.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly includes accompanying CITRs as part of the entire agreement, clarifying which disclosures are covered. The changed text omits the reference to CITRs, creating uncertainty and contradiction. The contradicted text defines confidential information based on the CITR form. Without the CITR reference in the 'entire agreement' clause, the scope of 'Confidential Information' becomes unclear, thus directly contradicting the section that relies on CITRs to define it. Therefore the omission creates a strong in-text contradiction, and a 'YES' is warranted.

perturbation.original_text: Title or the right to possess Confidential Information as between the parties shall remain in the disclosing party.
perturbation.changed_text: Title or the right to possess Confidential Information shall be determined by mutual agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall remain in the disclosing party' to 'shall be determined by mutual agreement' alters the fundamental principle of ownership. The original agreement clearly defines who owns what and this change creates ambiguity. It contradicts other sections that assume the disclosing party retains ownership. Removing the specifics of who owns the Confidential Information makes it an in-text contradiction, since earlier it was clearly stated who it belongs to. The new definition is contradictory to the whole agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The confidential, proprietary and trade secret information of the disclosing party (hereinafter "Confidential Information") provided hereunder, is that information described in the Confidential Information Transmittal Record (CITR) form executed from time to time hereafter. CITR's are subject to the terms of this Agreement and shall be executed by the parties prior to the disclosure of Confidential Information. All information described in a CITR and marked with a "confidential," "proprietary," or similar legend shall be deemed Confidential Information. All Confidential Information received from the disclosing party shall be in tangible form. The CITR shall set forth the disclosing party, a description of the Confidential Information disclosed, the names of the representatives of the parties and the date when the disclosure covered by the CITR commenced.
perturbation.justification: The original text clearly states that the disclosing party retains ownership of Confidential Information. The changed text replaces this definitive statement with 'shall be determined by mutual agreement,' introducing ambiguity and directly contradicting the contradicted text. The contradicted text assumes the disclosing party owns the information, which is now uncertain under the changed text, resulting in a strong in-text contradiction. The explanation provided also confirms that this is an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The receiving party shall maintain the Confidential Information with at least the same degree of care that the receiving party uses to protect its own similar categories of confidential and proprietary information, but no less than a reasonable degree of care under the circumstances.
perturbation.changed_text: The receiving party shall endeavor to maintain Confidential Information securely.
perturbation.explanation: By replacing the specific requirement for maintaining confidentiality (same degree of care as own information, but no less than reasonable) with the vague 'endeavor to maintain securely', this change weakens the obligation. This is an omission because the detailed requirement of care is omitted, making the agreement less enforceable. The phrase 'shall endeavor' means to try or attempt. 'Shall endeavor' doesn't set any requirements of the receiver party, allowing them to break the confidentality agreement. The original paragraph states that it 'shall' be no less than a reasonable degree of care under the circumstances, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The receiving party shall not disclose Confidential Information to any third party without the prior written approval of the disclosing party. The receiving party shall endeavor to maintain Confidential Information securely. The receiving party may make copies of Confidential Information as deemed necessary. The receiving party is encouraged to label the copies accordingly.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated a specific standard of care for Confidential Information ('same degree of care...but no less than a reasonable degree of care'). The changed text weakens this obligation to simply 'endeavor to maintain securely.' This change introduces a contradiction in the contradicted text where it mentions 'The receiving party shall not disclose Confidential Information to any third party...The receiving party shall endeavor to maintain Confidential Information securely.' Since the original text mandates a standard of care, and the changed text weakens it, this contradicts the original intent and creates ambiguity around the receiver's actual obligations, making enforcement difficult and leading to an in-text contradiction as defined in the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition, as the original strict obligation of care is omitted.
